sourceMovie.title: Mrs. Doubtfire
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Mrs. Doubtfire (1993) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Chris Columbus's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Uncle Buck
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Mrs. Doubtfire clicked for you, Uncle Buck (1989) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Despicable Me 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Mrs. Doubtfire clicked for you, Despicable Me 2 (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Mrs. Doubtfire?
faq.answer: Try Uncle Buck, Despicable Me 2, The Simpsons Movie—each shares DNA with Mrs. Doubtfire while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Mrs. Doubtfire?
faq.answer: Mrs. Doubtfire (1993) is best described as Comedy, Drama, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Mrs. Doubtfire worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
